questions.question: The Ghaggar River, that was recently seen in news, originates from which region?
questions.explanation: Recently, villages along the Ghaggar River in Haryana have reported rising cancer cases, allegedly linked to polluted river water. The Ghaggar River is an intermittent river, meaning it flows mainly during the monsoon season and remains dry for the rest of the year. It originates from Dagshai village in the Shivalik Hills of Himachal Pradesh at an elevation of about 1,927 metres above mean sea level. The river is called Ghaggar in India and Hakra in Pakistan, together known as the Ghaggar-Hakra system. It flows through Ambala and Hisar districts of Haryana and eventually dries up in the Thar Desert.

questions.question: Which institution has launched the third edition of VAANI (Vibrant Advocacy for Advancement and Nurturing of Indian Languages) scheme?

options.label: A
options.text: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)

options.label: C
options.text: National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)

answer.label: A
answer.text: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)

questions.explanation: The All India Council for Technical Education has launched the third edition of AICTE-VAANI (Vibrant Advocacy for Advancement and Nurturing of Indian Languages) to promote Indian languages in technical education. The scheme aims to create an inclusive knowledge ecosystem by enabling students to learn technical subjects in their mother tongue, reducing language barriers in higher education. It encourages research publication in regional languages and strengthens collaboration between academia and industry. Under the scheme, ₹4 crore annually will support 200 conferences, seminars, and workshops, with ₹2 lakh per institution.

questions.question: Hussain Sagar Lake is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Hussain Sagar Lake is facing severe foul odour due to heavy algal bloom during rising summer temperatures. Algal bloom occurs due to eutrophication, where excess nutrients like nitrogen and phosphorus enter the water. Hussain Sagar Lake is an artificial lake in Hyderabad, built in 1562 AD under Ibrahim Quli Qutb Shah. It is located on a tributary of the Musi River and is among Asia’s largest man-made lakes. The lake is also called Tank Bund and connects Hyderabad and Secunderabad.

questions.question: Which institute has established the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence for Artificial Intelligence-enabled geological and mining systems?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur has established the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence for Artificial Intelligence-enabled geological and mining systems. The centre is funded with ₹15 crore over five years by Vikram Sodhi, Vice Chairman of Mineros SA. It aims to develop Artificial Intelligence-based solutions tailored to Indian geological and mining conditions. The initiative focuses on integrating the entire mining value chain including exploration, planning, processing, safety, and environmental management.

questions.question: Kulsi River, that was recently seen in news, is a south-bank tributary of which river?
questions.explanation: A proposed hydropower project on the Kulsi River is facing opposition from local residents due to ecological concerns. The Kulsi River is a south-bank tributary of the Brahmaputra River. It originates from the West Khasi Hills of Meghalaya through three streams—Khri, Krishniya, and Umsiri. The river is called Khri in the upper reaches and later flows northwest, entering Assam at Ukium. It flows through Kamrup district and joins the Brahmaputra near Nagarbera.

questions.question: India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park is being established in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park is being established in Ladakh on the banks of the Indus River to preserve ancient rock art. Petroglyphs are prehistoric carvings or engravings on rock surfaces depicting symbols, figures, and inscriptions. The park aims to protect endangered petroglyphs threatened by tourism, infrastructure development, and environmental factors. It will relocate and conserve carvings from vulnerable and isolated sites across Ladakh for safe preservation and public display.

questions.question: SMILE scheme (Support for Marginalised Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise) was launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.explanation: The SMILE Scheme (Support for Marginalised Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise) has been allocated ₹390 crore by the Government of India for the period 2021–2026 to support marginalized groups. It was launched in 2022 by the Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. It focuses on addressing beggary mainly in religious, tourist, and historical cities. The scheme also includes a sub-scheme for the empowerment of transgender persons.

questions.question: Which ministry has introduced the use of Truck Mounted Attenuators?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways has introduced Truck Mounted Attenuators (TMA). A Truck Mounted Attenuator is a crash-absorbing safety device fitted on trucks in work zones. It absorbs and dissipates kinetic energy during collisions, reducing impact force. TMAs protect road construction and maintenance workers ahead of the vehicle. They also reduce injury risk for occupants of the colliding vehicle. These devices minimize accident severity on highways, especially in work zones.

questions.question: Which district of Maharashtra secured first rank in the country for awareness activities under the 8th National Nutrition Fortnight campaign?
questions.explanation: Ahilyanagar district ranked 1st in India for activities under the National Nutrition Fortnight campaign. It recorded 3,24,075 awareness activities, the highest in the country. Pune district secured 2nd position nationwide. The campaign is conducted by the Women and Child Development Department. It is part of the National Nutrition Fortnight (Poshan Pakhwada) initiative. The theme is “Brain Development in the First Six Years of a Child’s Life.”

questions.question: Sugor Tso Lake, that was recently seen in news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Sugor Tso is a high-altitude dual lake system located in the Upper Siang district, Arunachal Pradesh, within the Eastern Himalayan region. It consists of Upper Sugor Tso and Sugor Tso, which are hydrologically connected and fed by the Sugor stream. The lake lies in the Eastern Himalayan biodiversity hotspot, known for rich flora, fauna, and fragile ecosystems. It is formed in natural mountain depressions, receiving water from streams, rainfall, and snowmelt. Due to its remote location and difficult terrain, access is limited, which has helped preserve its natural condition.
